description: Project compiles with `npx tsx src/cli.ts` without errors (even if it does nothing)
steps: Run `npx tsx src/cli.ts` from the project root
Verify the process exits with code 0 (no TypeScript compilation errors)
Verify no import resolution errors appear in the output

description: Running `npx tsx src/cli.ts --help` shows usage information (using commander)
steps: Run `npx tsx src/cli.ts --help`
Verify output includes the program name 'ccclone'
Verify output includes the description of the program
Verify output lists the -p/--prompt option
Verify output lists the -V/--version option

description: Running `npx tsx src/cli.ts -p "hello"` accepts input (can print placeholder response for now)
steps: Run `npx tsx src/cli.ts -p "hello"`
Verify the process does not crash
Verify some output is printed acknowledging the prompt
Verify the process exits (does not hang waiting for input)

description: api.ts creates an Anthropic client using ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var
steps: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable to a valid key
Call createClient() and verify it returns an Anthropic instance
Verify the client is configured with the correct API key
Verify that calling createClient() without ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set throws or the SDK handles it

description: api.ts can send a simple message to Claude and get a response (no streaming, no tools)
steps: Create an Anthropic client with a valid API key
Call sendMessage() with a simple user message like 'Say hello'
Verify a response is returned with text content
Verify the response contains usage information (input_tokens, output_tokens)

description: The REPL in cli.ts reads user input, calls the API, and prints the raw response text
steps: Run `npx tsx src/cli.ts` (interactive mode) with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set
Type a simple message like 'hello' and press Enter
Verify the assistant's response is printed to the terminal
Verify you can type another message (REPL continues)
Exit with Ctrl+C and verify clean exit

description: agent.ts implements the agent loop: send message, if tool_use in response execute tool and re-send, if text only return
steps: Call runAgentLoop() with a simple text prompt (no tools needed)
Verify it returns a text response
Verify the returned AgentLoopResult includes usage information
Call runAgentLoop() with a prompt that would trigger tools (but with empty tool list)
Verify it returns a text response since no tools are available

description: The REPL uses the agent loop instead of direct API calls. Conversation history persists across turns.
steps: Run the REPL interactively
Send a message like 'My name is Alice'
Send a follow-up message like 'What is my name?'
Verify the assistant remembers the name (conversation history persists)
Verify the agent loop is being used (not direct API calls)

description: tools/read-file.ts defines the read_file tool schema and implements its executor
steps: Verify readFileTool.definition has correct name, description, and input_schema
Call readFileTool.execute({ file_path: 'package.json' })
Verify it returns the contents of package.json
Call readFileTool.execute({ file_path: 'nonexistent.txt' })
Verify it returns an error message (not throws/crashes)

description: tools/write-file.ts defines write_file tool schema and executor
steps: Verify writeFileTool.definition has correct name, description, and input_schema
Call writeFileTool.execute({ file_path: 'test-output/test.txt', content: 'hello world' })
Verify the file was created with the correct content
Verify parent directories were created automatically
Clean up test file after verification

description: tools/edit-file.ts defines edit_file tool schema and executor
steps: Create a test file with known content
Call editFileTool.execute({ file_path: 'test.txt', old_string: 'foo', new_string: 'bar' })
Verify the file content was updated correctly
Call editFileTool.execute() with an old_string that doesn't exist in the file
Verify it returns an error message indicating the string was not found

description: tools/bash.ts defines bash tool schema and executor
steps: Verify bashTool.definition has correct name, description, and input_schema
Call bashTool.execute({ command: 'echo hello' })
Verify it returns 'hello' (stdout output)
Call bashTool.execute({ command: 'ls nonexistent-dir' })
Verify it returns the stderr output without crashing

description: tools/index.ts exports a registry: array of tool definitions and executeTool dispatch function
steps: Call getToolDefinitions() and verify it returns an array of 4 tool definitions
Verify each definition has name, description, and input_schema fields
Call executeTool('read_file', { file_path: 'package.json' }) and verify it works
Call executeTool('unknown_tool', {}) and verify it throws an appropriate error

description: Tool definitions are passed to the API call in api.ts
steps: Verify that sendMessage() and streamMessage() accept a tools parameter
Verify that getToolDefinitions() output is compatible with the Anthropic API tool format
Make an API call with tools included and verify no errors from the API about tool format

description: Agent loop executes tools and appends tool_result messages correctly
steps: Run the REPL with all tools enabled
Ask 'read the file package.json'
Verify the agent uses the read_file tool
Verify the tool result (file contents) is sent back to Claude
Verify Claude responds with information about the file contents

description: api.ts supports streaming responses using client.messages.stream()
steps: Call streamMessage() with a simple prompt
Provide an onText callback that collects tokens
Verify the callback is called multiple times with text chunks
Verify the final assembled message matches the complete response

description: Streaming works for text-only responses (tokens appear one by one in terminal)
steps: Run the REPL interactively
Send a message that will produce a long text response
Verify tokens appear incrementally in the terminal (not all at once)
Verify the complete response is readable and correct

description: Streaming correctly handles tool_use blocks
steps: Run the REPL with streaming enabled
Ask a question that requires a tool call (e.g., 'read package.json')
Verify the tool_use block is accumulated correctly during streaming
Verify the tool is executed after the stream ends
Verify the tool result is sent back to the model

description: After a tool result is sent back, the next response also streams
steps: Run the REPL with streaming enabled
Ask a question that triggers a tool call
After the tool executes, verify the follow-up response also streams token by token
Verify the complete flow: stream -> tool call -> stream response is seamless

description: User input is prompted with a visible indicator (e.g., '> ' or 'You: ')
steps: Run the REPL interactively
Verify a prompt indicator is visible (e.g., '> ' or 'You: ')
Type a message and verify the indicator appears again after the response

description: Assistant responses are visually distinct from user input (different color)
steps: Run the REPL interactively
Send a message and observe the response
Verify the assistant's text is rendered in a different color than the user's input
Verify the color is applied using chalk

description: Markdown in responses is rendered in the terminal (headers, bold, italic, lists)
steps: Send a prompt that will produce markdown output (e.g., 'List 3 items with a header')
Verify headers are rendered with appropriate formatting
Verify bold and italic text are rendered differently
Verify bulleted/numbered lists are formatted properly
Verify the markdown rendering uses marked + marked-terminal

description: Code blocks in responses have syntax highlighting
steps: Send a prompt that will produce a code block (e.g., 'Write a hello world in Python')
Verify the code block is visually distinct from surrounding text
Verify syntax highlighting is applied (keywords, strings, etc. are colored)
Verify the language label is shown if provided in the markdown

description: A spinner/loading indicator shows while waiting for API response
steps: Run the REPL and send a message
Verify a spinner or loading indicator appears while waiting for the response
Verify the spinner disappears once the response starts streaming
Verify the spinner animation is smooth and doesn't flicker

description: Tool calls are displayed to the user (e.g., 'Reading file: package.json...')
steps: Run the REPL and trigger a tool call (e.g., 'read package.json')
Verify a message is displayed showing which tool is being called
Verify the tool call display includes relevant parameters (e.g., file path)
Verify the display is visually distinct from assistant text

description: Tool results are shown (or summarized) before the assistant's next response
steps: Run the REPL and trigger a tool call
Verify the tool result is displayed or summarized after the tool executes
Verify the result display is distinguishable from assistant text
Verify long results are truncated or summarized appropriately

description: context/manager.ts tracks the total token count of the conversation
steps: Create a ContextManager instance
Call updateUsage() with sample usage data
Call getUsage() and verify it returns correct cumulative totals
Call updateUsage() again and verify totals accumulate
Verify the agent loop calls updateUsage() after each API response

description: Token count is displayed somewhere visible (e.g., in the prompt line or status line)
steps: Run the REPL and send a few messages
Verify the token count is displayed somewhere visible after each response
Verify the count updates after each API call
Verify both input and output tokens are tracked

description: context/compaction.ts implements compaction: summarizes old messages and replaces them
steps: Create a conversation with several messages
Call compactMessages() with the messages array
Verify the returned array has fewer messages
Verify the first message is a summary of the old conversation
Verify the summary captures key information from the original messages

description: When token count exceeds 80% of context window, compaction is triggered automatically
steps: Simulate a conversation that reaches 80% of the context window
Verify ContextManager.needsCompaction() returns true
Verify the agent loop checks for compaction after each turn
Verify compaction is triggered automatically when the threshold is exceeded

description: After compaction, the conversation continues normally
steps: Trigger a compaction (or simulate one)
Send a new message after compaction
Verify the assistant responds normally
Verify the token count is lower after compaction
Verify the assistant still has context from the summary

description: -p 'prompt' mode: runs a single prompt through the agent loop, prints result, exits
steps: Run `npx tsx src/cli.ts -p 'What is 2+2?'` with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set
Verify the assistant's response is printed to stdout
Verify the process exits after printing the response (exit code 0)
Verify tools work in -p mode (e.g., -p 'Read the file package.json')

description: /help command in REPL shows available commands
steps: Run the REPL interactively
Type '/help' and press Enter
Verify a list of available commands is displayed
Verify the list includes /help and /clear at minimum
Verify the help text is formatted readably

description: /clear command starts a fresh conversation (resets messages array)
steps: Run the REPL and send a message like 'My name is Bob'
Type '/clear' and press Enter
Verify a confirmation message is shown
Send 'What is my name?' and verify the assistant does NOT remember 'Bob'
Verify the token count resets after /clear

description: Ctrl+C during generation cancels the current response gracefully
steps: Run the REPL and send a message that will produce a long response
Press Ctrl+C while the response is streaming
Verify the generation stops
Verify the REPL returns to the prompt (doesn't crash)
Verify you can send another message after cancellation

description: Missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY shows a helpful error message and exits
steps: Unset the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable
Run `npx tsx src/cli.ts`
Verify a clear error message is shown explaining the missing key
Verify the message suggests how to set the key
Verify the process exits with a non-zero exit code

description: API errors (rate limit, network failure) are caught and displayed as user-friendly messages
steps: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to an invalid value
Run the REPL and send a message
Verify the error is caught and displayed as a readable message (not a stack trace)
Verify the REPL continues running after the error (doesn't crash)
Verify you can try again after the error

description: Tool execution errors are caught and sent back to Claude as error tool_results
steps: Trigger a tool that will fail (e.g., read a nonexistent file)
Verify the error is caught and sent as a tool_result with is_error: true
Verify Claude receives the error and responds appropriately (e.g., 'The file was not found')
Verify the REPL continues normally after the tool error

description: Malformed tool call arguments are handled gracefully
steps: Simulate a tool call with invalid/missing arguments
Verify the error is caught and sent back as an error tool_result
Verify Claude is informed about the malformed arguments
Verify the agent loop continues processing

description: If edit_file tool's old_string is not found in the file, return an error
steps: Create a test file with known content
Call edit_file with an old_string that does not exist in the file
Verify it returns an error message (not success)
Verify the error message clearly states the string was not found
Verify the original file is unchanged
